basis: CIF NWE / Dated Brent physical proxy
note: Brent has pushed toward $86 (WTI ~$80), up about 13% on the week, as the U.S.–Iran conflict escalated for a fourth straight day. President Trump floated — then, under Gulf pressure, dropped — a 20% Hormuz cargo fee, while Washington keeps a full blockade on Iran-linked shipping and has pledged to escalate to Iranian power plants and bridges (energy targets 'for last'). Hormuz transits have collapsed to 4–12 a day against a ~138 norm, most crossing dark, with the JMIC threat level at SEVERE. Physical NWE differentials are firming with the benchmark, and refined-product (diesel and jet) tightness is acute — European diesel margins at a record after Russia's export ban and Ukrainian strikes on Russian refining. Iran is signalling a second front, a Houthi threat to Bab el-Mandeb. Editorial proxy, not a traded quote — indicative, and watch tanker-transit data over the rhetoric.
